message:
fix(termwiz): saturate ReadBuffer::advance / find_subsequence on out-of-range offsets

`ReadBuffer` is a public buffer type used by the termwiz input
parser. Two of its public methods had hidden preconditions that
panic on violation — a foot-gun for callers who compute the
offset from external input.

1. `advance(len)` — when `len > self.storage.len()`,
   `self.storage.len() - len` underflows (panics in debug, wraps in
   release) and then `rotate_left(len)` panics on the
   self.storage-length invariant. Fixed by saturating
   `len = len.min(self.storage.len())` — over-advancing now
   consumes the full buffer, matching the "consume as many as
   possible" semantics a defensive buffer API expects.

2. `find_subsequence(offset, needle)` — when `offset >
   self.storage.len()`, `&self.storage[offset..]` panics on the
   out-of-range slice. Fixed by returning `None` when offset is
   past the buffer end, matching the already-correct behaviour for
   an offset at the buffer end (searching an empty suffix yields
   None).

Current call sites (termwiz/src/input.rs:1412 and :1420) bound
their `len` through keymap depth / `len_utf8()` respectively, so
the panics are not reachable in practice today. The fix is
defensive hardening: a future caller that computes `len` or
`offset` from untrusted input now degrades gracefully instead of
crashing.

Adds 5 regression tests:
- advance_saturates_when_len_exceeds_buffer (usize::MAX)
- advance_saturates_just_past_buffer_end (len + 1)
- advance_on_empty_buffer_is_noop
- find_subsequence_offset_past_end_returns_none
- find_subsequence_offset_equal_to_len_returns_none_gracefully

Verified: 17/17 readbuf tests pass via
  rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/termwiz/Cargo.toml --lib readbuf
Domain: termwiz multi-pass bug hunting (pane 5).
